subject: fix(assets): replace/remove two corrupt GLBs + validate GLB headers in the gate

subject: ci: run full merge gate + harden actions; fix(assets): two corrupt GLBs + header validation (#83)
decision: the asset gate validates GLB integrity (header + length), not just presence, enforced in both the deploy script and a unit test.

subject: fix(e2e): make waitForFrames robust to rAF stalls during canvas teardown (CR-rAF)
perf(audio): cache one-shot Howls per variant file, stop per-play leak (CR-M1)
refactor(urlflags): extract URL parsers to a testable module (CR-F6)

overrides.reason: pure refactor of URL-query parsing out of Shell.tsx into a helper module; no rendering, layout, component tree, or styling is touched — the only Shell change is swapping inline parser bodies for imports, behavior identical, covered by the new urlFlags unit test.

subject: refactor: enable noUncheckedIndexedAccess + guard all 482 index sites (CR-TS1)
test(e2e): make the screenshot suite a real visual-regression gate (CR-C2)

overrides.reason: changes only the e2e test harness (assertion + committed baseline PNGs) and the directive — no game rendering, component, style, or asset code is touched; the captured output is identical, this just starts diffing it.

subject: ci(deploy): smoke-test the live Pages deploy post-release (CR-H1)
ci(android): build the release APK (R8/ProGuard) in CI (CR-H2)
perf(effects): convert ParticleBurstField to a single InstancedMesh (CR-H1perf 1/3)
perf(effects): convert ShellEject + BodyPart to InstancedMesh (CR-H1perf 2-3/3)
perf(ci): probe live Howl count in the perf snapshot (CR-H1perf step c)
perf(effects): DamageNumberField — fixed slot pool, no per-frame React render (CR-R1)
refactor(prng): brand the Seed type — mulberry32 rejects raw numbers (CR-TS4)
feat(prng): add family two-PRNG core + seedphrase (SEED1)
decision: bone-buster uses the family two-PRNG seedrandom model; the

subject: feat(prng): rewrite the whole map RNG onto the seedphrase model (SEED2)

overrides.reason: the canonical "?bonebusterSeed=12345" pose still resolves to a corridor map (the new CANONICAL_SEED_PHRASE also hashes to corridor), so the committed screenshot baselines are byte-unchanged — this is an RNG-plumbing rewrite that produces the same rendered output for the gated poses, verified by re-running --update-snapshots leaving the goldens untouched plus a live in-game capture.

subject: feat(landing): New Game seed-phrase pane (SEED3)

overrides.reason: the gated canonical poses are the main menu + in-game (both unchanged); the new SeedPane is a brand-new screen reached only by clicking through New Game → procedural, so it can't regress any committed baseline — its render was verified by a separate live capture of the pane.

subject: feat(prng): bury the event-PRNG seed in Preferences (SEED4)

overrides.reason: the gated canonical poses (main menu + in-game) are unchanged; this wires event-seed persistence + the randomize handler behind the SeedPane (a non-gated screen), produces no change to any committed screenshot baseline, and the randomize behavior was verified by a separate live capture asserting the phrase string changed.

subject: docs(prng): record the seedphrase model + verify the full flow (SEED5)
resolves: directive SEED5 — the whole SEED1-5 family-PRNG + seedphrase

subject: refactor(scatter): extract shared sampling primitives + unify id stride (CR-M1)
chore: P3 polish — prod-audit CI gate, mobile draw-call budget, doc nit (CR-poly)
test(audio): real-Howler ambient + music lifecycle tests (CR-F9)
refactor(engine): extract enemy projectiles to fix wrong-direction dep (CR-H1eng/H-3)
decision: enemy projectile sim lives in src/engine/projectiles.ts, not engine.ts
why: keeps engine.ts free of @ai imports so the dependency graph flows map-geometry → projectiles → ai in-direction

overrides.reason: pure dependency-direction refactor moving the enemy projectile sim into its own module with zero behavior change, plus a browser-test flake timeout fix — no rendering, UI, or asset code is touched and every gated screenshot baseline is byte-unchanged.

subject: refactor(engine): decompose engine.ts along type seams into 6 modules (CR-H1eng)
decision: src/engine is decomposed into mapTypes/gridGen/gridCollision/sectors/spawn/collisionAny/projectiles, no engine.ts barrel
why: each module owns one responsibility and the import graph is an explicit acyclic DAG; a re-export barrel would hide the dependency direction the H-3 fix was about

overrides.reason: pure type-seam decomposition of the sim engine with zero behavior change — generateMap, collision, raycast, spawn, and projectile math are byte-identical, the 5 canonical screenshot baselines pass unchanged, and no rendering, shader, or asset code is touched.

subject: refactor(scene): extract <ScatterFields> from BoneBusterScene (CR-H1scene step-a)
resolves: directive CR-H1scene step-a (full-review H-1 Scene decomposition)

overrides.reason: pure JSX relocation of the scatter-field cluster into a wrapper component with identical props and render order — the 5 canonical screenshot baselines pass byte-for-byte and no field's own rendering logic is touched.

subject: refactor(scene): extract <EntityMeshes> from BoneBusterScene (CR-H1scene step-b)
resolves: directive CR-H1scene step-b (full-review H-1 Scene decomposition

overrides.reason: pure JSX relocation of the enemy/pickup/barrel mesh maps into a wrapper with identical props, keys, and register callbacks — the 5 canonical screenshot baselines (incl. the full kill-everything playthrough) pass byte-for-byte and no entity mesh's own rendering is touched.

subject: refactor(scene): extract runSceneTick() — the 200-line main frame tick (CR-H1scene step-c)
decision: the main scene tick body lives in src/scene/tick/sceneTick.ts as runSceneTick(deps); the useFrame registration stays in BoneBusterScene
why: keeps frame-callback ordering byte-identical and avoids re-homing the shared-ref web a child component would have forced — decomposes the tick without relocating the God-component
resolves: directive CR-H1scene step-c (full-review H-1 Scene decomposition

overrides.reason: pure relocation of the per-frame tick body into a function the unchanged useFrame calls — frame-callback registration order is preserved, every gameplay computation is byte-identical, and the 5 canonical screenshot baselines (incl. the strobe + full-clear poses) pass unchanged.

subject: refactor(state): promote GameState to a pure gameReducer + dissolve flushSync (CR-H1scene step-d / CR-F8)
decision: GameState transitions live in a pure gameReducer returning effects-as-data; useGameRef is a thin adapter that runs it against stateRef.current and drains effects after setState — no flushSync
why: dissolves the mid-render-dispatch hazard at the root (pure reducer = no side effects in the updater), makes every transition unit-testable, and folds CR-F8's pickup table into the collectPickup arm

overrides.reason: state-management refactor moving GameState transitions into a pure reducer with zero rendering change, verified byte-for-byte by the five canonical screenshot baselines plus the real-mount Shell browser test.

subject: refactor(shell): extract useLevelTransition + break the Shell value-import cycle (CR-H1scene step-d)
decision: level-transition lifecycle lives in useLevelTransition; shared lifecycle constants live in @views/gameConstants (leaf)
why: groups the cohesive transition logic out of the Shell God-component and breaks the Shell↔reducer/hook value-import cycle at the root rather than tolerating it
resolves: directive CR-H1scene step-d (useLevelTransition extraction) — Scene + Shell halves now complete

overrides.reason: organizational extraction of the level-transition effect into a hook plus a constants-module move to break an import cycle — zero rendering change, the 5 canonical screenshot baselines pass byte-for-byte and the full-clear pose exercises the moved transition logic.

subject: feat(combat): deterministic pellet spread + extract onEnemyKilled (CR-F7)
decision: pellet spread is seeded per (map.seedPhrase, shotIndex) via forkStream("FIRE-<n>"); shell-eject + particle jitter stay Math.random
why: makes which-enemy-a-pellet-hits reproducible for deterministic combat tests + replay, while leaving render-only jitter unseeded since it can't affect the sim outcome

overrides.reason: seeds the pellet-spread RNG + extracts a kill-side-effects helper with identical dispatches in the same order — the rendered combat output is unchanged, the 5 canonical screenshot baselines pass byte-for-byte, and only sim-determinism (not visuals) is affected.

subject: fix(scene): render damage-number text without suspending the scene subtree (CR-e2e-hooks)
decision: damage-number text renders via a non-suspending troika <primitive>, not drei's suspend()-wrapped <Text>
why: drei <Text> suspends unconditionally on troika's font pipeline; under a worker fault that suspension blocks the whole BoneBusterScene subtree from committing its effects — a latent production hazard, fixed at the root by removing the suspension
resolves: directive CR-e2e-hooks (the troika-suspense root cause; sql-wasm + key-hook tail remains)

overrides.reason: swaps drei <Text> for the equivalent non-suspending troika primitive so the scene subtree commits without blocking on troika's font load — damage numbers render identically (the 5 canonical screenshot baselines pass byte-for-byte), only the suspension behavior changed.

subject: fix(review): address PR #84 threads — unused imports + per-frame troika re-layout

overrides.reason: the punch animation now scales the group instead of fontSize and the base size is set declaratively — the rendered damage numbers are visually identical (same tier sizes + punch-in feel), the 5 canonical screenshot baselines pass byte-for-byte, only the per-frame troika geometry rebuild is eliminated.

subject: fix(review): address valid CodeRabbit findings (PR #84)

overrides.reason: seed-input sanitizer + reroll archetype-preservation + event-seed race guard are pure input/state-plumbing logic with no rendering change, and the engine guards throw only on impossible/contract-violating inputs so the canonical screenshot baselines are unaffected.

subject: refactor(store): move gameConstants into src/store — fix the layer inversion (PREP-C2)

overrides.reason: pure constants-module relocation plus import-path repoint to fix a layer inversion, with no rendering or layout or asset change whatsoever; the app/views touch is one moved import line and a comment.

subject: refactor(store): extract domain types out of Shell into src/store/gameState.ts (PREP-C1)

overrides.reason: pure type-definition relocation from a UI file into src/store with all importers repointed — types are erased at build, no runtime/rendering/layout change, the canonical screenshots are unaffected.

subject: perf(spawn): pickUvHidden O(n²) → single-fork pickUvHiddenFlags (PREP-PERF2)

overrides.reason: byte-identical UV-hidden flag computation (single fork vs per-enemy re-fork) — same enemies hide for a given seed, the canonical screenshot short-circuits to false anyway, baselines unchanged; pure O(n²)→O(n) perf.

subject: perf(scene): gate VIS1 directional shadows off on native mobile (PREP-PERF3)
decision: Mobile drops real-time directional shadows; desktop web retains them.
why: ~4-8ms/frame Pixel-5a cost + doubled shadow-caster draws for no aesthetic gain on PSX assets, while desktop baselines must stay byte-identical.

subject: test(e2e): real in-game visual-regression baseline via scene-ready wait (VIS-AUTO)
decision: In-game visual regression is gated by a scene-ready luminance wait + preserveDrawingBuffer under the debug flag, not by frame counts.
why: The black-frame failure was a wall-clock/asset-stream timing race, not a GL-backend deadlock; waiting on real paint makes a headless Playwright capture render the lit scene and assert it.

subject: fix(scene): dispose WaterSurface DataTexture on unmount (PREP-BP1)
decision: WaterSurface owns and disposes its DataTexture.
why: A self-`new`-ed GPU texture is not covered by R3F's geometry auto-free; without disposal it leaks one texture per water sector per map load.

subject: refactor(store): add assertNever exhaustiveness on the union switches (PREP-BP2)
decision: Discriminated-union switches over GameAction/GameEffect use assertNever in default.
why: STRUCT4 will add variants; an unhandled case must fail the build, not return undefined / silently no-op at runtime.

subject: test(scene): enemy-bullet integration coverage in sceneTick (PREP-TEST1)
docs: align DESIGN/DECISIONS/ARCHITECTURE/README with OVERHAUL2 (PREP-DOC)
fix(scripts): perf + playtest scripts listened on the dead objexoom: event names (CI-12)
resolves: review CI-12 (+ the same dead-name class in pt6/publish).

subject: refactor(store): name the goal-bonus ammo + cap the seed-phrase URL (PREP-MISC: L-2, M-6/SEC-1)
decision: A seed phrase from the URL is capped at 200 chars (reject, not truncate).
why: The seed vocabulary is three short words; an over-long phrase is hostile input into the hash + every PRNG fork, with no legitimate use.

subject: fix(shell): audio init can no longer block game start; log silent pref failures (PREP-MISC: M-5, M-7)
chore(gates): ban dangerouslySetInnerHTML (PREP-MISC: CI-8)
docs(security): CSP posture decision + smoke-URL rationale (PREP-MISC: SEC-2, SEC-3)
ci(smoke): assert the live deploy canvas is laid out, not just present (PREP-MISC: CI-7, CI-5)
refactor(scatter): share bboxOf + document the stateRef HUD constraint (PREP-MISC: M-1, BP-3)
ci: SHA-pin all GitHub Actions (PREP-MISC: CI-9)
feat(perf): ?bonebusterNoShadows flag for the mobile-perf shadow A/B (PREP-CI3, partial)
resolves: review CI-3 (mechanism + flag; A/B-assertion tail tracked).

subject: fix(scene): run troika text on the main thread to kill the Vite worker crash (ERR2)
decision: troika typesetting runs on the main thread (useWorker:false), not its Web Worker.
why: Vite's dep optimizer mangles troika's worker init; main-thread typesetting is correct + cheap for our short cached strings and removes 48 console errors per mount.

subject: feat(scene): asset-load error modal + asset-error event + deploy gate (ERR1)
decision: A failed asset load surfaces a text-only modal + a bonebuster:assetError event, never a silent blank render.
why: review BP-6 (Suspense with no ErrorBoundary) + CI-10 (smoke test couldn't see a missing mesh); arcade-cabinet parity.

subject: feat(weapons): center-bottom weapon hold + fold ERR1 review findings (VIS4)
resolves: PRD VIS4; folds ERR1 review findings (classify port

subject: feat(scene): brighter, more readable flood + fog recede (VIS3)
decision: Flood floor raised to ambient 1.25 / hemisphere 0.95; fog near pushed to 18.
why: PSX assets must be clearly visible (user: brighter/readable, modernized DOOM); mood comes from fog depth + directional shape, not a dim base.
resolves: PRD VIS3 (+ the VIS1/VIS2 readability defects).

subject: feat(scene): swap TreasureChest + LockedDoor placeholders to PSX GLBs (VIS5)
decision: Decorative/interactive objects with a promoted PSX GLB render that GLB; objects with no matching GLB stay procedural until one is sourced.
why: OVERHAUL2 visual fidelity — but a wrong-shape stand-in is worse than a clean procedural silhouette for gameplay-readable pickups.
resolves: PRD VIS5 (TreasureChest + LockedDoor; remainder scoped + deferred with reason).

subject: feat(hud): own-only weapon bar — DOOM pickup model, not always-5 (HUD2)
decision: The HUD weapon bar shows only owned weapons, in order.
why: DOOM-style arsenal that grows on pickup reads better than an always-5 bar of mostly-disabled buttons (OVERHAUL2 HUD direction).

subject: feat(hud): responsive scene frame — chrome cockpit / phone vignette (HUD1)
decision: HUD scene frame is device-class responsive — chrome cockpit on tablet/unfolded/desktop, vignette on phones — led by Capacitor Device.getInfo().
why: a chrome surround frames a big screen as a cockpit but boxes in a phone; the real device (not a pixel ratio) is the correct signal, with viewport as the fold-aware secondary.

subject: fix(scene): LockedDoor initial position + refine STRUCT plan from review/user
resolves: VIS5 review (LockedDoor flash); records STRUCT design decisions.

subject: refactor(engine): extract representation-agnostic MazeGenerator core (STRUCT1a)
